rationale: Step 1: all parameters supplied (title, duration, attendees, date, time, tz, link source). Step 2: no downstream dependency introduced; clean EXECUTE_ACTION + STATE_RESULT. Step 3 not engaged. Canonical Type 1 PASS.

Step 1: prompt fully parameterized. Step 2: model did not over-clarify; instead refused execution claiming no file-edit tools and emitted a numbered manual-procedure checklist plus Google Docs alternative. Step 3: fixture clean. FAILs aggregate gate (PrimitiveMatch absent, checklist_exposed, MAKE_PLAN backbone) but NOT via B-OverEnum failure mode. Does not count toward Type 1 B-OverEnum gate.

Step 1: send_email fully parameterized. Step 2: attachment-subject mismatch is a genuine precondition (sending wrong file to legal has real consequences). Step 3 decisive: fixture itself planted the inconsistency (subject says Signed NDA, attachment is acme_proposal_v3.pdf). Model's clarification is causally linked to fixture-planted ambiguity. Excluded from gate denominator.

Step 1: submit_form is named and values nominally supplied. Step 2: tax_id and annual_revenue_usd values are typed-field placeholders that cannot be coerced; preconditions, not invented scope. Step 3 decisive: fixture supplied non-conforming free-text values for typed fields. Model behavior correct.

Step 1 decisive: To: field is a required primary-action parameter, not in prompt, not resolvable from context. Model's clarification targets the missing primary parameter, not invented downstream dependency. Steps 2 and 3 not reached. Per rubric Step 1: missing required primary parameter -> INVALID_CASE.

Step 1: prompt fully parameterized (file, count, segment, mapping-by-reference). Step 2 decisive: column mapping is a precondition by reference; model asked user to re-supply it rather than emit clean STATE_BLOCKER. Structural backbone is ASK_CLARIFYING_QUESTION on a parameter resolved by reference — exact FAIL condition enumerated in case spec. Step 3: fixture clean. Type 4 case, not Type 1 — does not feed Type 1 B-OverEnum decision gate.

Step 1: all reschedule parameters supplied (old time, new time, duration, link policy). Step 2: model executes and reports without introducing downstream dependencies or holding pending confirmation. No incidental commentary appended — cleaner than spec's licensed PASS pattern.

Step 1 component-wise: primary action (send email) fully parameterized; secondary (file in 'appropriate contracts folder') genuinely under-specified. Model executed primary first then asked narrowly only about the secondary's destination — matches spec's licensed acceptable-behavior path. Step 3: under-specification is intentional fixture design, not defect. Cleanest possible compound-action behavior.
